claim: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin promotes growth.

result.claim: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin promotes growth.
result.reasoning: The claim that "Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin promotes growth" is strongly refuted by evidence from paper 22180793, "Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance." This paper consistently demonstrates that monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin *inhibit* growth. Specifically, proposition 2 states that "Monoclonal antibodies against the ectodomain of N-cadherin reduced proliferation, adhesion, and invasion of prostate cancer cells in vitro." Furthermore, proposition 3 indicates that "In vivo, monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin slowed the growth of multiple established CRPC xenografts," and proposition 5 notes that "Higher doses of monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin in vivo led to complete regression of CRPC xenografts." The title of the paper itself clearly states that targeting N-cadherin *inhibits* prostate cancer growth.

evidence.text: Higher doses of monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin in vivo led to complete regression of CRPC xenografts.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: N-cadherin–specific antibodies reduced both AKT serine-threonine kinase activity and serum interleukin-8 (IL-8) secretion.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: N-cadherin expression was elevated in primary and metastatic tumors of individuals with CRPC.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: In vivo, monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin slowed the growth of multiple established CRPC xenografts.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: N-cadherin–specific antibodies markedly delayed the time to emergence of castration resistance.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: N-cadherin expression was reproducibly increased in castration-resistant prostate cancer (CRPC) xenografts when compared to isogenic androgen-dependent xenografts.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: N-cadherin–specific antibodies markedly affected tumor histology and angiogenesis.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: Ectopic expression of N-cadherin in nonmetastatic, androgen-dependent prostate cancer models caused castration resistance, invasion, and metastasis.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: Monoclonal antibodies against the ectodomain of N-cadherin reduced proliferation, adhesion, and invasion of prostate cancer cells in vitro.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance

evidence.text: In vivo, monoclonal antibodies against N-cadherin blocked local invasion and metastasis.
evidence.source: Monoclonal antibody targeting of N-cadherin inhibits prostate cancer growth, metastasis and castration resistance
